alert.reason: An official food safety alert is open, the response spans multiple jurisdictions, and source investigation is incomplete.

linkedOutbreak.product: Shredded iceberg lettuce from Taylor Farms de Mexico served at some Taco Bell locations

mapSignals.label: Michigan reported 2,640 state-level cases by July 13; state reports can include probable cases and may exceed the federal confirmed count.
An early Southeast Michigan cluster prompted local and state investigation and provider alerts.
Ohio is one of five states in the federally identified lettuce-linked outbreak.
Northwest Ohio is within the multi-jurisdiction response area; exact local counts should be checked with ODH.
Kentucky is one of five states in the federally identified lettuce-linked outbreak.
West Virginia is one of five states in the federally identified lettuce-linked outbreak.
Indiana reports increased cyclosporiasis activity and is included in the five-state federal outbreak.
Texas has reported 2026 cases, but this city marker is a monitoring signal and is not part of the five-state lettuce advisory.
Florida reports seasonal cases; this signal is not evidence of linkage to the five-state lettuce outbreak.
Mid-Atlantic cases have been publicly reported, but no common source is identified here.
Washington officials said they were not seeing a suspected common source or outbreak signal as of July 17.

news.title: Taylor Farms de Mexico to remove central-Mexico iceberg lettuce from the U.S. market
news.summary: Federal traceback converged on one supplier for the lettuce-linked subset. The broader national case total includes illnesses not tied to this outbreak.

news.title: CDC issues five-state food safety alert for shredded iceberg lettuce
news.summary: CDC advises people not to eat implicated shredded iceberg lettuce served at some Taco Bell locations in IN, KY, MI, OH, and WV.

news.title: Michigan updates prevention recommendations as reports rise
news.summary: Michigan reported 2,640 cases at the state level and reiterated the need for Cyclospora-specific testing.

blog.title: News in Reverse: start with the verified scope
blog.dek: Yesterday’s public feed contained 3 indexed item(s). The daily brief works backward from official scope before adding headlines.
paragraphs: The headline trail, newest first: The FDA Is Investigating New Cyclospora Outbreak, but Hasn’t Identified Source; Cyclospora in Maryland: Health officials report 110 cases since May 1 - WBAL-TV; ‘We Had It Coming’: Cyclosporiasis Outbreak Reveals Holes in Food Safety System.
The stable reference point remains 1,645 federally confirmed domestically acquired cases across 41 reporting states. State totals may be newer, broader, or include probable cases.

The current federal source finding applies to a large five-state lettuce-linked subset. Water remains a possible general transmission route, but the current CDC and FDA pages do not identify a municipal water system as this outbreak’s source.
blog.method: Automatically assembled from RSS headline metadata plus the latest saved CDC/FDA figures. It does not copy article bodies, diagnose illness, or convert headlines into case counts.
